name: Kiwi Daiquiri Jam
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons Rum (or substitute with fruit juice?)
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill a water canner with boiling water and place 4 clean half-pint mason jars inside.
2. Bring the water to a boil and maintain it for at least 10 minutes to sterilize jars at altitudes up to 1000 ft.
3. Place snap lids in boiling water and boil for 5 minutes to soften the sealing compound.
4. In a large stainless steel or enamel saucepan, mash kiwifruit to applesauce consistency.
5. Stir in sugar, pineapple juice, and lime juice. Bring mixture to a full rolling boil, stirring until sugar dissolves.
6. Continue boiling vigorously for 2 minutes without stopping.
7. Remove from heat and stir in pectin. Continue stirring for an additional 5 minutes to prevent floating fruit.
8. If desired, add green food coloring to achieve a vibrant, intensely green jam color.
9. Stir in rum.
10. Ladle hot jam into sterilized jars, leaving 1/4 inch of headspace at the rim. Remove air bubbles by gently pressing a rubber spatula between the glass and the filling, adjusting the headspace if needed.
11. Wipe jar rims clean to remove any residue. Center snap lids on jars and secure screw bands fingertip tight.
12. Place jars in the canner and return water to a boil. Process for 5 minutes at altitudes up to 1000 ft.
13. Remove jars from boiling water and allow them to cool undisturbed for 24 hours.
14. Check jar seals by pressing down on the lids; sealed lids will curve slightly downward. Store in a cool, dark place after sealing properly.
